title: ruellia nudiflora var. humilis
intro: Ruellia nudiflora var. humilis, also known as the Mexican petunia or wild petunia, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant native to Mexico and the southern United States. Traditionally, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are rich in antioxidants and have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Ruellia nudiflora var. humilis can be used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
